buildFlagsNote: Pre-2026-06-01 baselines (R4/R-May/R3) fail codesign: the current Local.xcconfig provisioning profile does not match bundle id 'app.prodcore.Prodcore' at those commits. Signing is orthogonal to dead-code analysis (a false positive is a COMPILE error, not a signing error), so all Prodcore builds disable code signing. R5 (post-06-01) compiled+signed fine; disabling signing changes nothing there.

fixesMade.summary: forceRemoveAll deleted 4 files pinned via PBXFileSystemSynchronizedBuildFileExceptionSet.membershipExceptions (ProductType/DocumentOperations/IconReference/RegionHelpers) → 'Build input files cannot be found'. Fixed: parseSynchronizedMembershipExceptions unions sync-group exceptions into the not-safe-to-delete set so they are shelled, not deleted. Treeswift rebuilt, dylib verified fresh.

After F18, forceRemoveAll left exactly 4 'cannot declare a public initializer in an extension with internal requirements' (ProductionInspector/ProductionToolbar/FastPickerView/GridPickerView): a public generic type was downgraded to internal but its constrained-extension public init was not. Fixed: computeBatchModifications records publicDowngradedTypeNames; cascadePublicStripFromExtensions strips leading public from those types' extension members. Unused-only removal was already clean (257 deleted, 0 errors), proving the core claim independent of this access-control cascade.
fixesMade.fixture: TODO (public generic type + constrained-extension public init)

fixesMade.summary: R3 full removal (after unused pass) left 1 error: 'method must be declared fileprivate because its result uses a fileprivate type' (SequenceView:724). Top-level struct SectionGroup was narrowed to fileprivate but func computeSectionGroups() -> [SectionGroup] in 'extension Array where ...' was not cascaded (existing cascade only covers same-parent-scope nested types). Fixed: record all fileprivate-narrowed type names; cascadeFileprivateToReferencingFunctions does a file-wide pass inserting fileprivate on any no-access-keyword func whose signature references a fileprivate type. Unused-only removal on R3 was already clean (849 deleted, 0 errors). F17/F19/F20 are one access-control-cascade family.
fixesMade.fixture: TODO (top-level fileprivate type returned by an extension method)

findings.summary: ScanCache re-serves already-fixed redundant-accessibility warnings as no-op ghosts after in-place access-keyword rewrites; fixed only by hard-deleting scan-cache-<UUID>.json (app killed) before a cold rescan. Not a false positive (build clean), not a Periphery bug. Memory: scancache-stale-accesscontrol-ghosts.
findings.actionable: Invalidate a file's cached warnings when an in-place access rewrite touches it.

passes.note: redunAcc 5 residual was stale ScanCache; cold rescan = 0

baselines.result: CONVERGED: unused 271->0, redunAcc 464->0, 776 decls removed, build clean every pass, 0 false positives. Floor 5 assignOnly.
CONVERGED: unused 272->0, redunAcc 465->0, ~828 decls removed, build clean every pass, 0 false positives. Floor 5 assignOnly. Cold-cache discipline avoided the phantom-ghost detour.
